diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest tightness is common in panic from hyperventilation and chest-wall tension, but is non-specific.
Acute chest pressure is a cardinal ACS symptom that must be excluded before attribution to anxiety.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dyspnoea / cannot catch a full breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Air-hunger and hyperventilation are core panic symptoms.
Acute dyspnoea is a leading PE symptom requiring risk assessment and, if indicated, imaging.
Catecholamine-driven sinus tachycardia and ectopy are typical in panic.
A subjective 'skipping' rhythm cannot be characterised without an ECG; arrhythmia must be excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset during intense online health-information reading (cyberchondria trigger)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear psychological precipitant tightly time-locked to symptom onset strongly supports a panic mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sense of impending doom / fear of dying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hallmark cognitive feature of panic attacks.

symptoms.symptom_name: No radiation of chest discomfort to arm or jaw

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of radiation lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it.
Absence of syncope/haemoptysis lowers the probability of a high-risk PE.

symptoms.symptom_name: No PE risk factors (no immobilisation, travel, surgery, or calf swelling)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low Wells-type risk factor burden reduces PE pre-test probability.
Low-titer ANA is common and frequently insignificant; a weak, non-specific pointer that functions as a red herring here.
Raynaud phenomenon with low-titer ANA is consistent with mild UCTD, but is not the cause of tonight's acute event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-reported heart rate ~108 on a smartwatch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tachycardia is expected in panic; a consumer-wearable rate is patient-obtainable and admissible in telehealth but unverified and cannot characterise rhythm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular sinus rhythm without murmurs, rubs or gallops on cardiac examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a pericardial friction rub argues against pericarditis — a clinician-only auscultation finding, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally with good air entry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear lungs neither confirm nor exclude PE, but reassure against pneumonia/effusion — a clinician-only auscultation finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mildly reproducible chest-wall tenderness on palpation, pain not worse with exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: musculoskeletal chest wall pain (costochondritis / muscle tension)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible palpation tenderness supports a musculoskeletal contributor — requires clinician palpation, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured SpO2 100% on room air and BP 140/85

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal oxygenation lowers concern for a significant PE — but this is a clinician measurement; the patient in this encounter is not offered an oximeter, so it stays sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG (not performed; would be required to exclude ACS)

investigation_details.expected_result: expected normal sinus tachycardia without ischaemic changes
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without an ECG, acute coronary syndrome and significant arrhythmia cannot be excluded — the single most important reason this presentation needs in-person assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal ECG (with serial troponin as indicated) is the key step to safely exclude ACS and permit a confident panic diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Panic constellation with clear psychological precipitant
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Time-locked onset to health-anxiety rumination plus the autonomic and cognitive features together make panic the leading diagnosis — but only after organic mimics are excluded in person.
Negative red-flag screen lowers the probability of the can't-miss diagnoses but, being history-only, cannot substitute for an ECG and examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The pivotal test to exclude ACS and characterise the palpitations — the main reason telehealth must escalate to in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinician vital signs including oxygen saturation and blood pressure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objective observations to support risk stratification, obtainable only in person in this encounter.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum troponin (if ACS features or ECG changes)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Adjunct to exclude myocardial injury when the clinical picture or ECG warrants it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical CTPA / D-dimer without PE risk assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Low PE pre-test probability (no risk factors, no syncope/haemoptysis) means empirical PE imaging is not indicated; a risk-stratified approach avoids over-investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Extended autoimmune serology panel driven by the low-titer ANA
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The ANA is a benign red herring for tonight's acute event; chasing it urgently would be anchoring and over-investigation.
